title: Being right is allowed to matter less
subtitle: An affirmation for choosing peace over the argument
excerpt: For the conflict where you are probably right, and staying in it is costing you more than letting it be unresolved. A statement for the choice to value your own peace over the conclusion.

practice.whenToUse: When a conflict or grievance is ongoing and the cost of continuing to pursue resolution outweighs the value of the resolution

content.text: Sometimes the thing worth releasing is not the grievance itself but the requirement to be acknowledged as right about it. The grievance may be entirely valid. The other person may have wronged you. And still, the pursuit of resolution, the replaying of arguments, the waiting for an acknowledgement, can cost more than the grievance is worth.
This affirmation is for the conscious choice to value your own peace over the conclusion.
Being right is allowed to matter less.
Then ask: what would I do differently today if I had already chosen to let this go? Start there.
